headline.purpose: Balances, totals, page titles — the numbers that matter most.

title.purpose: Card headers, dialog titles, section labels.

label.purpose: Badges, table headers, small status tags.

shadows.name: floating
shadows.purpose: Dialogs, popovers, anything portaled above the page.

components.name: Primary Button
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 40px; padding: 8px 16px; background: #6467f2; color: #ffffff; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; border-radius: 6px; border: none; transition: background-color 0.15s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: rgba(100, 103, 242, 0.9); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #111218, 0 0 0 4px #6467f2; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Lower-emphasis action alongside a primary button.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 40px; padding: 8px 16px; background: #2e3148; color: #e1e7ef; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; border-radius: 6px; border: none; transition: background-color 0.15s; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: rgba(46, 49, 72, 0.8); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 40px; padding: 8px 16px; background: #111218; color: #e1e7ef; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; border-radius: 6px; border: 1px solid #2e3148; transition: background-color 0.15s; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: #2e3148; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard text/number field used across forms.
components.css: .ds-input { display: flex; height: 40px; width: 100%; border-radius: 6px; border: 1px solid #2e3148; background: #111218; color: #e1e7ef; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 14px; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #94a3b8; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #111218, 0 0 0 4px #6467f2; }

components.name: Default Badge
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; border-radius: 9999px; padding: 2px 10px; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; background: #6467f2; color: #ffffff; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Base container for dashboard summaries, list panels, form sections.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #181b25; color: #e1e7ef; border: 1px solid #2e3148; border-radius: 8px; padding: 24px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 rgb(0 0 0 / 0.05); } .ds-card-title { font-size: 14px; color: #94a3b8; margin-bottom: 8px; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 24px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.02em; }

narrative.overview: The interface reads like a bank statement checked at night: a dim, near-black surface where the only bright thing in the room is the number that matters. Signal Indigo is the single focal accent — it marks the primary action, the active focus ring, the one data point the eye should land on — and everything else recedes into quiet neutral tones. This is a shared household ledger, not a consumer growth app: there are no badges, no confetti, no mascots, and no cheerful SaaS-cream marketing gloss. It is also not a dense trading terminal; density stays casual, legible at a glance by any family member, not optimized for a power user staring at it all day.
keyCharacteristics: Dark-only surface (no light theme); depth built from tone steps, not brightness jumps
One accent color (Signal Indigo) used sparingly, for action and focus only
Flat cards distinguished from the page by a single lightness step, not a border-heavy grid
Inter throughout, no display face — data is the hierarchy, not typographic flourish

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Signal Indigo appears only on primary actions, focus/ring states, and active selection. If more than one element on a screen is indigo at rest, it's being used wrong.

rules.name: The Numbers-First Rule
rules.body: The largest, boldest type on any screen is always a balance, delta, or total — never a section heading or a piece of UI chrome.

rules.name: The Two-Step Rule
rules.body: There are exactly two elevation levels: resting (shadow-sm) and floating (shadow-lg). Nothing in between; don't invent a third shadow weight.

dos: Do keep Signal Indigo (#6467f2) to primary actions, focus rings, and active/selected state only.
Do separate cards from the page with the Ledger Card tone step (#181b25 on #111218) plus the ambient shadow, not a heavier border.
Do keep every loading/error/empty state explicit per project convention — never show ambiguous or stale numbers.
Do use the same component pattern (same button variant, same badge shape) for the same kind of action across Accounts, Transactions, Categories, Goals, and Tags.
donts: Don't add playful/gamified fintech flourishes — badges-as-achievements, confetti, mascots, or celebratory animation.
Don't push toward a dense trading-terminal look (cramped multi-panel data grids, monospace-everything).
Don't drift toward a cheerful SaaS-cream marketing aesthetic (light warm-neutral hero sections, gradient text, eyebrow labels).
Don't introduce a second saturated accent color competing with Signal Indigo; Alert Red is destructive-only.
Don't render muted-foreground gray (#94a3b8) at a size/weight that drops body text below 4.5:1 contrast against the background.
